title: Model types: ODE, DDE, stochastic, and discrete
text: programmatic manipulation dynamical systems (rather code generation), algebraicodin provides ResourceSharer Machine classes following AlgebraicDynamics.jl: Evaluate vector field given state:
code: rs <- petri_to_continuous(sir) rs@system_type #> [1] "continuous" rs@nstates #> [1] 3 rs@state_names #> [1] "S" "I" "R" u <- c(990, 10, 0) p <- list(inf = 0.0005, rec = 0.25) eval_dynamics(rs, u, p, t = 0) #> [1] -4.95  2.45  2.50

title: Model types: ODE, DDE, stochastic, and discrete
text: Convert continuous system discrete via forward Euler:
code: rs_disc <- euler_approx(rs, h = 0.1) rs_disc@system_type #> [1] "discrete"  # One Euler step u_next <- eval_dynamics(rs_disc, u, p, t = 0) cat("u_next:", u_next, "\n") #> u_next: 989.505 10.245 0.25 cat("u + 0.1 * f(u):", u + 0.1 * eval_dynamics(rs, u, p, t = 0), "\n") #> u + 0.1 * f(u): 989.505 10.245 0.25

what: All four types from one Petri net
title: Model types: ODE, DDE, stochastic, and discrete
code: # Run all four model types run_model <- function(code, pars, times, type) {   gen <- odin2::odin(code)   is_discrete <- type %in% c("Stochastic", "Discrete")   args <- list(generator = gen, pars = pars, n_particles = 1L,                deterministic = (type != "Stochastic"), seed = 42L)   if (is_discrete) args$dt <- 1   sys <- do.call(dust2::dust_system_create, args)   dust2::dust_system_set_state_initial(sys)   y <- dust2::dust_system_simulate(sys, times)   data.frame(time = times, I = y[2, ], type = type) }  pars_cont <- list(inf = 0.0005, rec = 0.25, S0 = 990, I0 = 10, R0 = 0) pars_disc <- pars_cont  dde_delays <- list(inf = list(tau = 5, species = "I"))  df_all <- rbind(   run_model(pn_to_odin(sir, "ode"), pars_cont, t_ode, "ODE"),   run_model(pn_to_odin(sir, "dde", delays = dde_delays), pars_cont, t_ode, "DDE"),   run_model(pn_to_odin(sir, "stochastic"), pars_disc, t_disc, "Stochastic"),   run_model(pn_to_odin(sir, "discrete"), pars_disc, t_disc, "Discrete") ) #> ℹ Using cached generator #> ℹ Using cached generator #> ℹ Using cached generator #> ℹ Using cached generator  ggplot(df_all, aes(time, I, colour = type)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(title = "SIR model: four types from one Petri net",        x = "Time", y = "Infectious (I)", colour = "Model type") +   theme_minimal()

title: Model types: ODE, DDE, stochastic, and discrete
text: Petri net single source truth. Changing model type one-line change pn_to_odin(sir, type = ...), stoichiometry remains consistent across four formulations.

title: More model examples
text: vignette demonstrates additional epidemiological models beyond basic SIR, illustrating flexibility algebraicodin’s stock-flow framework. model built, compiled via odin2, simulated.
code: library(algebraicodin) #>  #> Attaching package: 'algebraicodin' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     %o%, %x% library(odin2) library(dust2)

title: More model examples
text: Ross-Macdonald model describes malaria-like transmission humans mosquitoes. Humans cycle Susceptible → Infected → Recovered, mosquitoes go Susceptible → Infected (recovery). Mosquitoes birth death dynamics. Key features demonstrated: - Two host populations (humans mosquitoes) single stock-flow model - NULL flows birth (inflow nothing) death (outflow nothing) - Multiple sum variables separate population totals Parameters: - — mosquito biting rate - b — probability human infection per bite - c_param — probability mosquito infection per bite infected human - r — human recovery rate - mu — mosquito birth/death rate (1/lifespan) Ross-Macdonald vector-borne disease dynamics human population conserved (human demographics), mosquito population reaches steady state births deaths:
code: ross_mac <- stock_and_flow(   stocks = c("Sh", "Ih", "Rh", "Sv", "Iv"),   flows = list(     # Human disease dynamics     human_inf = flow(from = "Sh", to = "Ih",       rate = a * b * Iv / Nh * Sh),     human_rec = flow(from = "Ih", to = "Rh", rate = r * Ih),     # Mosquito infection     mosq_inf = flow(from = "Sv", to = "Iv",       rate = a * c_param * Ih / Nh * Sv),     # Mosquito demographics     mosq_birth = flow(from = NULL, to = "Sv", rate = mu * Nm),     mosq_death_s = flow(from = "Sv", to = NULL, rate = mu * Sv),     mosq_death_i = flow(from = "Iv", to = NULL, rate = mu * Iv)   ),   sums = list(     Nh = c("Sh", "Ih", "Rh"),     Nm = c("Sv", "Iv")   ),   params = c("a", "b", "c_param", "r", "mu") ) cat(sf_to_odin(ross_mac, type = "ode")) #> a <- parameter() #> b <- parameter() #> c_param <- parameter() #> r <- parameter() #> mu <- parameter() #>  #> Sh0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(Sh) <- Sh0 #> Ih0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(Ih) <- Ih0 #> Rh0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(Rh) <- Rh0 #> Sv0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(Sv) <- Sv0 #> Iv0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(Iv) <- Iv0 #>  #> Nh <- Sh + Ih + Rh #> Nm <- Sv + Iv #>  #> v_human_inf <- a * b * Iv/Nh * Sh #> v_human_rec <- r * Ih #> v_mosq_inf <- a * c_param * Ih/Nh * Sv #> v_mosq_birth <- mu * Nm #> v_mosq_death_s <- mu * Sv #> v_mosq_death_i <- mu * Iv #>  #> deriv(Sh) <- - v_human_inf #> deriv(Ih) <- v_human_inf - v_human_rec #> deriv(Rh) <- v_human_rec #> deriv(Sv) <- - v_mosq_inf + v_mosq_birth - v_mosq_death_s #> deriv(Iv) <- v_mosq_inf - v_mosq_death_i gen <- sf_to_odin_system(ross_mac, type = "ode") sys <- dust_system_create(gen(), list(   a = 0.3, b = 0.5, c_param = 0.5, r = 0.01, mu = 0.1,   Sh0 = 990, Ih0 = 10, Rh0 = 0, Sv0 = 5000, Iv0 = 100 )) #> ✔ Wrote 'DESCRIPTION' #> ✔ Wrote title: More model examples
text: SEIR model adds Exposed compartment (latent period becoming infectious). Adding birth death processes creates endemic equilibrium disease persists indefinitely. demographics, births balance deaths total population stays roughly constant, disease reaches endemic equilibrium: SEIR demographics — epidemic settles endemic state
code: seir_demo <- stock_and_flow(   stocks = c("S", "E", "I", "R"),   flows = list(     exposure = flow(from = "S", to = "E", rate = beta * S * I / N),     progression = flow(from = "E", to = "I", rate = sigma * E),     recovery = flow(from = "I", to = "R", rate = gamma * I),     birth = flow(from = NULL, to = "S", rate = mu * N),     death_S = flow(from = "S", to = NULL, rate = mu * S),     death_E = flow(from = "E", to = NULL, rate = mu * E),     death_I = flow(from = "I", to = NULL, rate = mu * I),     death_R = flow(from = "R", to = NULL, rate = mu * R)   ),   sums = list(N = c("S", "E", "I", "R")),   params = c("beta", "sigma", "gamma", "mu") ) gen <- sf_to_odin_system(seir_demo, type = "ode") sys <- dust_system_create(gen(), list(   beta = 0.5, sigma = 0.2, gamma = 0.1, mu = 0.01,   S0 = 990, E0 = 0, I0 = 10, R0 = 0 )) #> ✔ Wrote 'DESCRIPTION' #> ✔ Wrote 'NAMESPACE' #> ✔ Wrote 'R/dust.R' #> ✔ Wrote 'src/dust.cpp' #> ✔ Wrote 'src/Makevars' #> ℹ 13 functions decorated with title: More model examples
text: SIS model, recovered individuals return directly susceptible pool. creates endemic equilibrium * = N(1 − γ/β) R₀ = β/γ > 1. SIS model converges endemic equilibrium
code: sis <- stock_and_flow(   stocks = c("S", "I"),   flows = list(     infection = flow(from = "S", to = "I", rate = beta * S * I / N),     recovery = flow(from = "I", to = "S", rate = gamma * I)   ),   sums = list(N = c("S", "I")),   params = c("beta", "gamma") ) gen <- sf_to_odin_system(sis, type = "ode") sys <- dust_system_create(gen(), list(   beta = 0.5, gamma = 0.1,   S0 = 990, I0 = 10 )) title: More model examples
text: Stratification can model multiple strains sharing susceptible pool. strain independent infection recovery dynamics: Two competing strains different transmission rates
code: sir <- stock_and_flow(   stocks = c("S", "I", "R"),   flows = list(     infection = flow(from = "S", to = "I", rate = beta * S * I / N),     recovery = flow(from = "I", to = "R", rate = gamma * I)   ),   sums = list(N = c("S", "I", "R")),   params = c("beta", "gamma") )  sir_2strain <- sf_stratify(sir, c("strain1", "strain2"),   flow_types = c(infection = "disease", recovery = "disease")) code <- sf_to_odin(sir_2strain, type = "ode") cat(code) #> beta <- parameter() #> gamma <- parameter() #>  #> S_strain10 <- parameter(0) #> initial(S_strain1) <- S_strain10 #> S_strain20 <- parameter(0) #> initial(S_strain2) <- S_strain20 #> I_strain10 <- parameter(0) #> initial(I_strain1) <- I_strain10 #> I_strain20 <- parameter(0) #> initial(I_strain2) <- I_strain20 #> R_strain10 <- parameter(0) #> initial(R_strain1) <- R_strain10 #> R_strain20 <- parameter(0) #> initial(R_strain2) <- R_strain20 #>  #> N_strain1 <- S_strain1 + I_strain1 + R_strain1 #> N_strain2 <- S_strain2 + I_strain2 + R_strain2 #>  #> v_infection_id_strain1 <- beta * S_strain1 * I_strain1/N_strain1 #> v_infection_id_strain2 <- beta * S_strain2 * I_strain2/N_strain2 #> v_recovery_id_strain1 <- gamma * I_strain1 #> v_recovery_id_strain2 <- gamma * I_strain2 #>  #> deriv(S_strain1) <- - v_infection_id_strain1 #> deriv(S_strain2) <- - v_infection_id_strain2 #> deriv(I_strain1) <- v_infection_id_strain1 - v_recovery_id_strain1 #> deriv(I_strain2) <- v_infection_id_strain2 - v_recovery_id_strain2 #> deriv(R_strain1) <- v_recovery_id_strain1 #> deriv(R_strain2) <- v_recovery_id_strain2 gen <- sf_to_odin_system(sir_2strain, type = "ode") sys <- dust_system_create(gen(), list(   beta = 0.3, gamma = 0.1,   S_strain10 = 990, S_strain20 = 990,   I_strain10 = 10, I_strain20 = 5,   R_strain10 = 0, R_strain20 = 0 )) #> ✔ Wrote 'DESCRIPTION' #> ✔ Wrote 'NAMESPACE' #> ✔ Wrote 'R/dust.R' #> ✔ Wrote 'src/dust.cpp' #> ✔ Wrote 'src/Makevars' #> ℹ 13 functions title: More model examples
text: base model can compiled stochastic discrete-time model Binomial transitions: Stochastic SIR — 20 realizations
code: gen_stoch <- sf_to_odin_system(sir, type = "stochastic") n_reps <- 20 t <- seq(0, 200) I_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_reps, ncol = length(t))  for (k in seq_len(n_reps)) {   sys <- dust_system_create(gen_stoch(), list(     beta = 0.3, gamma = 0.1,     S0 = 990, I0 = 10, R0 = 0   ), n_particles = 1, dt = 0.25)   dust_system_set_state_initial(sys)   res <- dust_system_simulate(sys, t)   st <- dust_unpack_state(sys, res)   I_matrix[k, ] <- as.numeric(st$I) } #> ✔ Wrote 'DESCRIPTION' #> ✔ Wrote 'NAMESPACE' #> ✔ Wrote 'R/dust.R' #> ✔ Wrote 'src/dust.cpp' title: More model examples
text: vignette demonstrated: Vector-borne disease (Ross-Macdonald) — two host populations, birth/death dynamics SEIR demographics — endemic equilibrium birth/death balance SIS — immunity, analytical endemic equilibrium * = N(1 − γ/β) Multi-strain — stratification competing pathogen variants Stochastic ensembles — discrete-time Binomial transitions Predator-prey (Lotka-Volterra) — non-epidemiological application Waning immunity (SIRS) — recurrent epidemic dynamics models follow workflow: define stock_and_flow(), generate code sf_to_odin() sf_to_odin_system(), simulate dust2.

title: Stock-flow stratification
text: Stratification key technique epidemiological modeling adds heterogeneity model subdividing populations subgroups (strata). example, SIR model can stratified age group capture age-dependent transmission recovery. algebraicodin package implements stock-flow stratification following approach StockFlow.jl. based typed product (pullback) stock-flow models: given base disease model strata model, typed common type template, product creates valid pairs stocks flows.

title: Stock-flow stratification
text: Let’s start basic SIR model stratify two age groups.
code: library(algebraicodin) #>  #> Attaching package: 'algebraicodin' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     %o%, %x% library(ggplot2)

title: Stock-flow stratification
code: sir <- stock_and_flow(   stocks = c("S", "I", "R"),   flows = list(     infection = flow(from = "S", to = "I", rate = beta * S * I / N),     recovery = flow(from = "I", to = "R", rate = gamma * I)   ),   sums = list(N = c("S", "I", "R")),   params = c("beta", "gamma") )  plot_stock_flow(sir)

title: Stock-flow stratification
text: simplest case replicates disease dynamics within stratum, movement strata: stratified model 6 stocks (3 disease states × 2 age groups) 4 flows (2 disease flows × 2 age groups). Parameters shared across strata.
code:
sir_2age <- sf_stratify(sir, c("young", "old"),   flow_types = c(infection = "disease", recovery = "disease"))  cat("Stocks:", paste(sf_snames(sir_2age), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Stocks: S_young, S_old, I_young, I_old, R_young, R_old cat("Flows:", paste(sf_fnames(sir_2age), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Flows: infection_id_young, infection_id_old, recovery_id_young, recovery_id_old cat("Params:", paste(sf_pnames(sir_2age), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Params: beta, gamma plot_stock_flow(sir_2age)

what: Generate and simulate the odin2 model
title: Stock-flow stratification
code: cat(sf_to_odin(sir_2age, type = "ode")) #> beta <- parameter() #> gamma <- parameter() #>  #> S_young0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(S_young) <- S_young0 #> S_old0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(S_old) <- S_old0 #> I_young0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(I_young) <- I_young0 #> I_old0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(I_old) <- I_old0 #> R_young0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(R_young) <- R_young0 #> R_old0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(R_old) <- R_old0 #>  #> N_young <- S_young + I_young + R_young #> N_old <- S_old + I_old + R_old #>  #> v_infection_id_young <- beta * S_young * I_young/N_young #> v_infection_id_old <- beta * S_old * I_old/N_old #> v_recovery_id_young <- gamma * I_young #> v_recovery_id_old <- gamma * I_old #>  #> deriv(S_young) <- - v_infection_id_young #> deriv(S_old) <- - v_infection_id_old #> deriv(I_young) <- v_infection_id_young - v_recovery_id_young #> deriv(I_old) <- v_infection_id_old - v_recovery_id_old #> deriv(R_young) <- v_recovery_id_young #> deriv(R_old) <- v_recovery_id_old gen <- sf_to_odin_system(sir_2age, type = "ode") sys <- dust2::dust_system_create(gen(), list(   beta = 0.3, gamma = 0.1,   S_young0 = 500, S_old0 = 400,   I_young0 = 5, I_old0 = 5,   R_young0 = 0, R_old0 = 0 )) #> ── R CMD INSTALL ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> * installing *source* package ‘odin.systemef03de17’ ... #> ** this is package title: Stock-flow stratification
text: many applications, individuals move strata. age stratification, means aging: individuals “young” group transition “old” rate. Now 7 flows: 4 disease flows (infection recovery stratum) plus 3 aging flows (one per disease compartment: S, , R).
code:
sir_age <- sf_stratify(sir, c("young", "old"),   flow_types = c(infection = "disease", recovery = "disease"),   cross_strata_flows = list(     list(name = "aging", from = "young", to = "old",          rate = quote(aging_rate * young), params = c("aging_rate"))   ) )  cat("Stocks:", paste(sf_snames(sir_age), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Stocks: S_young, S_old, I_young, I_old, R_young, R_old cat("Flows:", paste(sf_fnames(sir_age), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Flows: infection_id_young, infection_id_old, recovery_id_young, recovery_id_old, aging_S, aging_I, aging_R cat("Params:", paste(sf_pnames(sir_age), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Params: beta, gamma, aging_rate plot_stock_flow(sir_age)

title: Stock-flow stratification

") #> Stocks: S_child, S_adult, S_senior, I_child, I_adult, I_senior, R_child, R_adult, R_senior cat("Flows:", paste(sf_fnames(sir_3age), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Flows: infection_id_child, infection_id_adult, infection_id_senior, recovery_id_child, recovery_id_adult, recovery_id_senior, aging_ca_S, aging_as_S, aging_ca_I, aging_as_I, aging_ca_R, aging_as_R cat("Num flows:", length(sf_fnames(sir_3age)), "

title: Stock-flow stratification
text: Stock-flow stratification works base model. ’s SEIR × 2 age groups:
code: seir <- stock_and_flow(   stocks = c("S", "E", "I", "R"),   flows = list(     exposure = flow(from = "S", to = "E", rate = beta * S * I / N),     infection = flow(from = "E", to = "I", rate = "old",          rate = quote(aging_rate * young), params = c("aging_rate"))   ) )  cat("Stocks:", length(sf_snames(seir_age)), "\n") #> Stocks: 8 cat("Flows:", length(sf_fnames(seir_age)), "\n") #> title: Stock-flow stratification
text: verify stratification, let’s compare hand-written model:
code: manual_code <- " beta <- parameter() gamma <- parameter()  S_a0 <- parameter(0) I_a0 <- parameter(0) R_a0 <- parameter(0) S_b0 <- parameter(0) I_b0 <- parameter(0) R_b0 <- parameter(0)  initial(S_a) <- S_a0 initial(I_a) <- I_a0 initial(R_a) <- R_a0 initial(S_b) <- S_b0 initial(I_b) <- I_b0 initial(R_b) <- R_b0  N_a <- S_a + I_a + R_a N_b <- S_b + I_b + R_b  deriv(S_a) <- -beta * S_a * I_a / N_a deriv(I_a) <- beta * S_a * I_a / N_a - gamma * I_a deriv(R_a) <- gamma * I_a deriv(S_b) <- -beta * S_b * I_b / N_b deriv(I_b) <- beta * S_b * I_b / N_b - gamma * I_b deriv(R_b) <- gamma * I_b "  # Algebraic version sir_2 <- sf_stratify(sir, c("a", "b"),   flow_types = c(infection = "disease", recovery = "disease"))  gen_alg <- sf_to_odin_system(sir_2, type = "ode") gen_man <- function() odin2::odin(manual_code)  pars <- list(beta = 0.3, gamma = 0.1,              S_a0 = 500, I_a0 = 5, R_a0 = 0,              S_b0 = 400, I_b0 = 5, R_b0 = 0)  sys_alg <- title: Stock-flow stratification
text: advanced use cases, can work directly typed stock-flow API:
code:
type_sf <- sf_infectious_type() cat("Type stocks:", sf_snames(type_sf), "
") #> Type stocks: pop cat("Type flows:", sf_fnames(type_sf), "
") #> Type flows: disease strata cat("Type params:", sf_pnames(type_sf), "
") #> Type params: rate tsf <- sf_typed(sir, type_sf,   stock_types = c(S = "pop", I = "pop", R = "pop"),   flow_types = c(infection = "disease", recovery = "disease"),   param_types = c(beta = "rate", gamma = "rate"))  tsf_r <- sf_add_reflexives(tsf, list(S = "strata", I = "strata", R = "strata")) cat("After adding reflexives:",     paste(sf_fnames(tsf_r@sf), collapse = ", "), "
") #> After adding reflexives: infection, recovery, refl_S_strata, refl_I_strata, refl_R_strata

title: Stock-flow stratification
text: Stock-flow stratification algebraicodin: sf_stratify() — high-level convenience function Supports arbitrary number strata Cross-strata flows (e.g., aging) rate expressions Parameters remain independent (cross-multiplied) Generates correct odin2 code simulation Based typed product (pullback) StockFlow.jl Lower-level API advanced use: sf_typed() — create typed stock-flow models sf_add_reflexives() — add identity flows stratification sf_typed_product() — compute pullback directly sf_infectious_type() — standard epidemiological type template

title: Spatial multi-patch models
text: Spatial heterogeneity important epidemiological modeling: disease dynamics can differ locations due population density, contact patterns, movement regions. Multi-patch (metapopulation) models capture dividing population discrete spatial units connected migration. algebraicodin, spatial patches modeled form stratification — patch runs disease dynamics, migration cross-strata flows optional cross-patch transmission via contact matrix. sf_spatial() convenience function wraps pattern.
code: library(algebraicodin) library(ggplot2) library(odin2) library(dust2)

title: Spatial multi-patch models
code: sir <- stock_and_flow(   stocks = c("S", "I", "R"),   flows = list(     infection = flow(from = "S", to = "I", rate = beta * S * I / N),     recovery = flow(from = "I", to = "R", rate = gamma * I)   ),   sums = list(N = c("S", "I", "R")),   params = c("beta", "gamma") )

title: Spatial multi-patch models
text: sf_spatial() takes base model, patch names, migration specs. contact parameter adds contact matrix cross-patch transmission: model : - 6 stocks: S, , R per patch - 10 flows: 4 disease (infection + recovery × 2 patches) + 6 migration (S, , R × 2 directions) - 6 params: gamma, migration rates, 4 contact matrix entries
code:
sir_2patch <- sf_spatial(sir, c("urban", "rural"),   migration = list(     mig_ur = list(from = "urban", to = "rural",                   rate = quote(m_ur * urban), params = "m_ur"),     mig_ru = list(from = "rural", to = "urban",                   rate = quote(m_ru * rural), params = "m_ru")   ),   contact = "C" )  cat("Stocks:", paste(sf_snames(sir_2patch), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Stocks: S_urban, S_rural, I_urban, I_rural, R_urban, R_rural cat("Flows:", length(sf_fnames(sir_2patch)), "
") #> Flows: 10 cat("Params:", paste(sf_pnames(sir_2patch), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Params: C_rural_rural, C_rural_urban, m_ur, m_ru, C_urban_urban, C_urban_rural

title: Spatial multi-patch models
code: plot_stock_flow(sir_2patch)

title: Spatial multi-patch models
code: cat(sf_to_odin(sir_2patch, type = "ode")) #> C_rural_rural <- parameter() #> C_rural_urban <- parameter() #> m_ur <- parameter() #> m_ru <- parameter() #> C_urban_urban <- parameter() #> C_urban_rural <- parameter() #>  #> S_urban0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(S_urban) <- S_urban0 #> S_rural0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(S_rural) <- S_rural0 #> I_urban0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(I_urban) <- I_urban0 #> I_rural0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(I_rural) <- I_rural0 #> R_urban0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(R_urban) <- R_urban0 #> R_rural0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(R_rural) <- R_rural0 #>  #> N_urban <- S_urban + I_urban + R_urban #> N_rural <- S_rural + I_rural + R_rural #>  #> lambda_infection_urban <- C_urban_urban * I_urban / N_urban + C_urban_rural * I_rural / N_rural #> lambda_infection_rural <- C_rural_urban * I_urban / N_urban + C_rural_rural * I_rural / N_rural #> lambda_recovery_urban <- C_urban_urban * R_urban + C_urban_rural * R_rural #> lambda_recovery_rural <- C_rural_urban * R_urban + C_rural_rural * R_rural #>  #> v_infection_id_urban <- lambda_infection_urban * S_urban #> v_infection_id_rural <- lambda_infection_rural * S_rural #> v_recovery_id_urban <- lambda_recovery_urban * I_urban #> v_recovery_id_rural <- lambda_recovery_rural * I_rural #> v_mig_ur_S <- m_ur * S_urban #> v_mig_ru_S <- m_ru * S_rural #> v_mig_ur_I <- m_ur * I_urban #> v_mig_ru_I <- m_ru * I_rural #> v_mig_ur_R <- m_ur * R_urban #> v_mig_ru_R <- m_ru * R_rural #>  #> deriv(S_urban) <- - v_infection_id_urban - v_mig_ur_S + v_mig_ru_S #> deriv(S_rural) <- - v_infection_id_rural + v_mig_ur_S - v_mig_ru_S #> deriv(I_urban) <- v_infection_id_urban - v_recovery_id_urban - v_mig_ur_I + v_mig_ru_I #> deriv(I_rural) <- v_infection_id_rural - v_recovery_id_rural + v_mig_ur_I - v_mig_ru_I #> deriv(R_urban) <- v_recovery_id_urban - v_mig_ur_R + v_mig_ru_R #> deriv(R_rural) <- v_recovery_id_rural + v_mig_ur_R - v_mig_ru_R

what: Simulate: outbreak seeded in one patch
title: Spatial multi-patch models
text: Disease starts urban patch spreads rural patch via migration cross-patch contact:  urban patch experiences epidemic first; rural patch follows delayed, smaller epidemic driven migration cross-patch transmission.

title: Spatial multi-patch models
text: Higher migration rates produce earlier larger epidemics rural patch.
code: run_model <- function(m_rate) {   sys <- dust_system_create(gen(), list(     gamma = 0.1, m_ur = m_rate, m_ru = m_rate,     C_urban_urban = 0.3, C_urban_rural = 0.05,     C_rural_urban = 0.05, C_rural_rural = 0.25,     S_urban0 = 990, I_urban0 = 10, R_urban0 = 0,     S_rural0 = 500, I_rural0 = 0, R_rural0 = 0   ))   dust_system_set_state_initial(sys)   res <- dust_system_simulate(sys, times)   dust_unpack_state(sys, res) }  states <- lapply(c(0.001, 0.01, 0.05), run_model)  df_mig <- rbind(   data.frame(time = times, I = states[[1]]$I_rural,              migration = "m = 0.001"),   data.frame(time = times, I = states[[2]]$I_rural,              migration = "m = 0.01"),   data.frame(time = times, I = states[[3]]$I_rural,              migration = "m = 0.05") )  ggplot(df_mig, aes(x = time, y = I, colour = migration)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(title = "Rural patch infection by migration rate",        subtitle = "Outbreak seeded in urban patch only",        x = "Time", y = "Infected (rural)") +   theme_minimal()

title: Spatial multi-patch models
text: sf_spatial() convenience wrapper. full control, use sf_stratify():
code: sir_direct <- sf_stratify(sir, c("p1", "p2"),   flow_types = c(infection = "disease", recovery = "disease"),   cross_strata_flows = list(     list(name = "mig_12", from = "p1", to = "p2",          rate = quote(m * p1), params = "m"),     list(name = "mig_21", from = "p2", to = "p1",          rate = quote(m * p2), params = "m")   ),   mixing = list(infection = "C") )  cat("Equivalent result:", length(sf_fnames(sir_direct)), "flows\n") #> Equivalent result: 10 flows

title: Spatial multi-patch models
text: Key points: - Patches treated strata stratification framework - Migration = cross-strata flows (apply compartments) - Contact matrix = cross-patch transmission weighting - Works base model (SIR, SEIR, etc.) - Generates compilable odin2 code (ODE, stochastic, discrete)

title: Stock-and-flow models
text: Stock--flow diagrams widely used formalism system dynamics, epidemiological modeling, ecological modeling. represent systems : Stocks — state variables accumulate time (e.g., susceptible, infected, recovered populations) Flows — rates change stocks (e.g., infection rate, recovery rate) Auxiliary variables — intermediate computations used flows Sum variables — aggregates stocks (e.g., total population N) Parameters — external constants (e.g., transmission rate beta) algebraicodin’s stock-flow support inspired StockFlow.jl uses categorical framework — stock-flow diagrams ACSets specific schema.
code: library(algebraicodin) #>  #> Attaching package: 'algebraicodin' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     %o%, %x% library(acsets) #>  #> Attaching package: 'acsets' #> The following object is masked from 'package:graphics': #>  #>     arrows #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     objects library(catlab) #>  #> Attaching package: 'catlab' #> The following object is masked from 'package:algebraicodin': #>  #>     typed_product library(ggplot2)

title: Stock-and-flow models
text: stock_and_flow() function provides declarative interface building stock-flow models. Flow rates specified quoted R expressions: Let’s inspect model:
code: sir <- stock_and_flow(   stocks = c("S", "I", "R"),   flows = list(     inf = list(from = "S", to = "I", rate = quote(beta * S * I / N)),     rec = list(from = "I", to = "R", rate = quote(gamma * I))   ),   params = c("beta", "gamma"),   sums = list(N = c("S", "I", "R")) ) sf_snames(sir)     # Stocks #> [1] "S" "I" "R" sf_fnames(sir)     # Flows #> [1] "inf" "rec" sf_pnames(sir)     # Parameters #> [1] "beta"  "gamma" sf_svnames(sir)    # Sum variables #> [1] "N" sf_vnames(sir)     # Auxiliary variables (auto-generated from flows) #> [1] "v_inf" "v_rec"

title: Stock-and-flow models
text: Blue boxes stocks, yellow circles flows, green diamonds sum variables. Solid arrows show material flow; dashed lines show information dependencies.
code: plot_stock_flow(sir)

title: Stock-and-flow models
text: inflow/outflow structure can extracted matrices:
code:
tm <- sf_transition_matrices(sir) cat("Inflow matrix (flow × stock):
") #> Inflow matrix (flow × stock): print(tm$inflow) #>     S I R #> inf 0 1 0 #> rec 0 0 1 cat("
Outflow matrix (flow × stock):
") #>  #> Outflow matrix (flow × stock): print(tm$outflow) #>     S I R #> inf 1 0 0 #> rec 0 1 0 cat("
Stoichiometry (stock × flow):
") #>  #> Stoichiometry (stock × flow): print(tm$stoichiometry) #>   inf rec #> S  -1   0 #> I   1  -1 #> R   0   1

title: Stock-and-flow models
text: simulate_sf() function provides convenient wrapper around deSolve:
code: result <- simulate_sf(sir,   initial = c(S = 990, I = 10, R = 0),   times = seq(0, 100, by = 0.5),   params = list(beta = 0.4, gamma = 0.2) )  head(result) #>   time        S        I        R #> 1  0.0 990.0000 10.00000 0.000000 #> 2  0.5 987.9221 11.02737 1.050568 #> 3  1.0 985.6362 12.15499 2.208815 #> 4  1.5 983.1233 13.39150 3.485195 #> 5  2.0 980.3629 14.74601 4.891048 #> 6  2.5 977.3332 16.22815 6.438651 df <- tidyr::pivot_longer(result, -time, names_to = "compartment",                            values_to = "count") ggplot(df, aes(x = time, y = count, color = compartment)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "SIR model (stock-and-flow)",        x = "Time", y = "Population") +   theme_minimal()

title: Stock-and-flow models
text: control, extract vectorfield function (compatible deSolve’s ode()):
code:
vf <- sf_vectorfield(sir) state <- c(S = 990, I = 10, R = 0) parms <- list(beta = 0.4, gamma = 0.2) du <- vf(0, state, parms)[[1]]  cat("At (S=990, I=10, R=0):
") #> At (S=990, I=10, R=0): cat("  dS/dt =", du["S"], "
") #>   dS/dt = -3.96 cat("  dI/dt =", du["I"], "
") #>   dI/dt = 1.96 cat("  dR/dt =", du["R"], "
") #>   dR/dt = 2

title: Stock-and-flow models
text: Stock-flow models can exported odin2 high-performance simulation via dust2.

title: Stock-and-flow models
code: code_ode <- sf_to_odin(sir, type = "ode",                         initial = c(S = 990, I = 10, R = 0)) cat(code_ode) #> beta <- parameter() #> gamma <- parameter() #>  #> S0 <- parameter(990) #> initial(S) <- S0 #> I0 <- parameter(10) #> initial(I) <- I0 #> R0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(R) <- R0 #>  #> N <- S + I + R #>  #> v_inf <- beta * S * I/N #> v_rec <- gamma * I #>  #> deriv(S) <- - v_inf #> deriv(I) <- v_inf - v_rec #> deriv(R) <- v_rec

title: Stock-and-flow models
code: code_stoch <- sf_to_odin(sir, type = "stochastic",                           initial = c(S = 990, I = 10, R = 0)) cat(code_stoch) #> beta <- parameter() #> gamma <- parameter() #>  #> S0 <- parameter(990) #> initial(S) <- S0 #> I0 <- parameter(10) #> initial(I) <- I0 #> R0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(R) <- R0 #>  #> N <- S + I + R #>  #> v_inf <- beta * S * I/N #> v_rec <- gamma * I #>  #> n_inf <- Binomial(S, min(1, v_inf / S * dt)) #> n_rec <- Binomial(I, min(1, v_rec / I * dt)) #>  #> update(S) <- S - n_inf #> update(I) <- I + n_inf - n_rec #> update(R) <- R + n_rec

title: Stock-and-flow models
code: code_disc <- sf_to_odin(sir, type = "discrete",                          initial = c(S = 990, I = 10, R = 0)) cat(code_disc) #> beta <- parameter() #> gamma <- parameter() #>  #> S0 <- parameter(990) #> initial(S) <- S0 #> I0 <- parameter(10) #> initial(I) <- I0 #> R0 <- parameter(0) #> initial(R) <- R0 #>  #> N <- S + I + R #>  #> v_inf <- beta * S * I/N #> v_rec <- gamma * I #>  #> update(S) <- S + (- v_inf) * dt #> update(I) <- I + (v_inf - v_rec) * dt #> update(R) <- R + (v_rec) * dt

title: Stock-and-flow models
text: Stock-flow diagrams naturally handle complex models:
code: seir <- stock_and_flow(   stocks = c("S", "E", "I", "R"),   flows = list(     inf = list(from = "S", to = "E", rate = quote(beta * S * I / N)),     prog = list(from = "E", to = "I", rate = quote(sigma * E)),     rec = list(from = "I", to = "R", rate = quote(gamma * I))   ),   params = c("beta", "sigma", "gamma"),   sums = list(N = c("S", "E", "I", "R")) )  result_seir <- simulate_sf(seir,   initial = c(S = 990, E = 0, I = 10, R = 0),   times = seq(0, 200, by = 0.5),   params = list(beta = 0.4, sigma = 0.2, gamma = 0.1) )  df_seir <- tidyr::pivot_longer(result_seir, -time, names_to = "compartment",                                 values_to = "count") ggplot(df_seir, aes(x = time, y = count, color = compartment)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "SEIR model (stock-and-flow)",        x = "Time", y = "Population") +   theme_minimal()

what: Open-population model with births and deaths
title: Stock-and-flow models
text: External inflows (births) outflows (deaths) specified = NULL = NULL:  Notice system reaches endemic equilibrium, unlike closed SIR always reaches disease-free.
code: sir_open <- stock_and_flow(   stocks = c("S", "I", "R"),   flows = list(     birth   = list(from = NULL,  to = "S",  rate = quote(mu * N)),     inf     = list(from = "S",   to = "I",  rate = quote(beta * S * I / N)),     rec     = list(from = "I",   to = "R",  rate = quote(gamma * I)),     death_S = list(from = "S",   to = NULL,  rate = quote(mu * S)),     death_I = list(from = "I",   to = NULL,  rate = quote(mu * I)),     death_R = list(from = "R",   to = NULL,  rate = quote(mu * R))   ),   params = c("beta", "gamma", "mu"),   sums = list(N = c("S", "I", "R")) )  result_open <- simulate_sf(sir_open,   initial = c(S = 900, I = 100, R = 0),   times = seq(0, 500, by = 1),   params = list(beta = 0.4, gamma = 0.2, mu = 0.01) )  df_open <- tidyr::pivot_longer(result_open, -time, names_to = "compartment",                                 values_to = "count") ggplot(df_open, aes(x = time, y = count, color = compartment)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Open-population SIR (births and deaths)",        x = "Time", y = "Population") +   theme_minimal()

title: Stock-and-flow models
code: plot_stock_flow(sir_open)

title: Stock-and-flow models
text: stock-flow model can converted Petri net (vice versa):
code:
# Stock-flow -> Petri net pn <- sf_to_petri(sir) cat("Petri net: ", acsets::nparts(pn, "S"), "species,",     acsets::nparts(pn, "T"), "transitions
") #> Petri net:  3 species, 2 transitions  # Check dynamics agree (mass-action, no sum variables) sir_ma <- stock_and_flow(   stocks = c("S", "I", "R"),   flows = list(     inf = list(from = "S", to = "I", rate = quote(beta * S * I)),     rec = list(from = "I", to = "R", rate = quote(gamma * I))   ),   params = c("beta", "gamma") )  sir_pn <- labelled_petri_net(   c("S", "I", "R"),   inf = c("S", "I") %=>% c("I", "I"),   rec = "I" %=>% "R" )  state <- c(S = 990, I = 10, R = 0)  # Stock-flow vectorfield vf_sf <- sf_vectorfield(sir_ma) du_sf <- vf_sf(0, state, list(beta = 0.0004, gamma = 0.2))[[1]]  # Petri net vectorfield vf_pn <- vectorfield(sir_pn) du_pn <- vf_pn(0, state, list(inf = 0.0004, rec = 0.2))[[1]]  cat("Stock-flow: ", du_sf, "
") #> Stock-flow:  -3.96 1.96 2 cat("Petri net:  ", du_pn, "
") #> Petri net:   -3.96 1.96 2 cat("Max diff:   ", max(abs(du_sf - du_pn)), "
") #> Max diff:    0

title: Stock-and-flow models
text: Stock-flow models can converted ResourceSharers use oapply_dynam(): means can mix stock-flow ResourceSharer components single UWD composition.
code: rs <- sf_to_resource_sharer(sir) rs@nstates #> [1] 3 rs@system_type #> [1] "continuous" rs@state_names #> [1] "S" "I" "R"  # Evaluate dynamics du <- rs@dynamics(   c(S = 990, I = 10, R = 0),   list(beta = 0.4, gamma = 0.2),   0 ) cat("du:", du, "\n") #> du: -3.96 1.96 2

title: Stock-and-flow models
text: Models can multiple independent sums:
code: two_patch <- stock_and_flow(   stocks = c("S1", "I1", "S2", "I2"),   flows = list(     inf1 = list(from = "S1", to = "I1",                 rate = quote(beta * S1 * I1 / N1)),     inf2 = list(from = "S2", to = "I2",                 rate = quote(beta * S2 * I2 / N2))   ),   params = c("beta"),   sums = list(     N1 = c("S1", "I1"),     N2 = c("S2", "I2")   ) )  result_2p <- simulate_sf(two_patch,   initial = c(S1 = 90, I1 = 10, S2 = 95, I2 = 5),   times = seq(0, 50, by = 0.1),   params = list(beta = 0.5) )  df_2p <- tidyr::pivot_longer(result_2p, -time, names_to = "state",                               values_to = "count") ggplot(df_2p, aes(x = time, y = count, color = state)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Two-patch SI model",        x = "Time", y = "Population") +   theme_minimal()

title: Stock-and-flow models
text: Stock--flow models algebraicodin provide: Declarative construction — stock_and_flow() quoted R expressions rates Automatic sum variables — total population N computed fly Three simulation modes — ODE, stochastic, discrete via odin2 Interoperability — convert /Petri nets ResourceSharers Visualization — DiagrammeR diagrams stock-flow structure Categorical foundation — ACSets composition via UWDs

title: Stratification with typed products
text: Many epidemiological models stratify base compartmental model attributes like age, risk group, vaccination status. hand error-prone: SIR model stratified 3 age groups 9 species many transitions track. algebraicodin automates stratification using typed Petri nets typed product, porting approach AlgebraicPetri.jl. categorical product slice category Petri/T\mathrm{Petri}/T, TT type system (ontology) specifies allowed transition types.
code: library(algebraicodin) #>  #> Attaching package: 'algebraicodin' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     %o%, %x%

title: Stratification with typed products
text: type system Petri net defines abstract transition patterns. infectious_ontology() provides standard type system infectious disease models: ontology one species type (Pop) three transition types:
code: ont <- infectious_ontology() species_names(ont) #> [1] "Pop" transition_names(ont) #> [1] "infect"  "disease" "strata"

title: Stratification with typed products
text: typed Petri net morphism ϕ:P→T\phi: P \T concrete Petri net type system. species transition PP assigned type TT. SIR species map Pop (index 1). inf transition maps infect (index 1) rec maps disease (index 2).
code: sir <- labelled_petri_net(   c("S", "I", "R"),   inf = c("S", "I") %=>% c("I", "I"),   rec = "I" %=>% "R" ) sir_typed <- typed_petri(sir, ont,   species_types    = c(S = "Pop", I = "Pop", R = "Pop"),   transition_types = c(inf = "infect", rec = "disease") ) sir_typed@species_map #> [1] 1 1 1 sir_typed@transition_map #> [1] 1 2 plot_petri(sir_typed)

title: Stratification with typed products
text: taking typed product, model must augmented reflexive transitions—identity self-loops allow product pair “real” transitions one model “-nothing” transitions . stratification age, SIR model needs reflexive strata transitions (aging can paired compartment): augmented model now 5 transitions:
code: sir_aug <- add_reflexives(sir_typed, list(   S = "strata",   I = "strata",   R = "strata" )) transition_names(sir_aug@pn) #> [1] "inf"           "rec"           "refl_S_strata" "refl_I_strata" #> [5] "refl_R_strata" plot_petri(sir_aug)

title: Stratification with typed products
text: Age groups modelled simple Petri net strata-typed transitions. two age groups aging: age model also needs reflexive transitions infect disease processes can occur within age group:
code: age <- labelled_petri_net(   c("Young", "Old"),   aging = "Young" %=>% "Old" ) age_typed <- typed_petri(age, ont,   species_types    = c(Young = "Pop", Old = "Pop"),   transition_types = c(aging = "strata") ) age_aug <- add_reflexives(age_typed, list(   Young = c("infect", "disease"),   Old   = c("infect", "disease") )) transition_names(age_aug@pn) #> [1] "aging"              "refl_Young_infect"  "refl_Young_disease" #> [4] "refl_Old_infect"    "refl_Old_disease" plot_petri(age_aug)

title: Stratification with typed products
text: typed product (stratification) creates valid pairs species transitions. %x% operator shorthand typed_product(): stratified model 6 species (S, , R age group) 7 transitions (within-group infection, cross-group infection, recovery group, plus age transitions). result : 6 species: S×{Young,Old}, ×{Young,Old}, R×{Young,Old} 7 transitions: infection recovery within age group, plus aging across compartment
code: sir_age <- sir_aug %x% age_aug species_names(sir_age) #> [1] "S_Young" "S_Old"   "I_Young" "I_Old"   "R_Young" "R_Old" transition_names(sir_age) #> [1] "inf_Young" "inf_Old"   "rec_Young" "rec_Old"   "aging_S"   "aging_I"   #> [7] "aging_R" plot_petri(sir_age)

title: Stratification with typed products
text: transition rate uses mass-action kinetics within appropriate stratum. aging transitions move individuals age groups preserving disease state.
code: code <- pn_to_odin(sir_age, "ode") cat(code) #> ## Auto-generated by algebraicodin #>  #> ## Parameters #> inf_Young <- parameter() #> inf_Old <- parameter() #> rec_Young <- parameter() #> rec_Old <- parameter() #> aging_S <- parameter() #> aging_I <- parameter() #> aging_R <- parameter() #>  #> ## Initial conditions #> S_Young0 <- parameter() #> S_Old0 <- parameter() #> I_Young0 <- parameter() #> I_Old0 <- parameter() #> R_Young0 <- parameter() #> R_Old0 <- parameter() #>  #> initial(S_Young) <- S_Young0 #> initial(S_Old) <- S_Old0 #> initial(I_Young) <- I_Young0 #> initial(I_Old) <- I_Old0 #> initial(R_Young) <- R_Young0 #> initial(R_Old) <- R_Old0 #>  #> ## Transition rates #> rate_inf_Young <- inf_Young * S_Young * I_Young #> rate_inf_Old <- inf_Old * S_Old * I_Old #> rate_rec_Young <- rec_Young * I_Young #> rate_rec_Old <- rec_Old * I_Old #> rate_aging_S <- aging_S * S_Young #> rate_aging_I <- aging_I * I_Young #> rate_aging_R <- aging_R * R_Young #>  #> ## Derivatives #> deriv(S_Young) <- -rate_inf_Young - rate_aging_S #> deriv(S_Old) <- -rate_inf_Old + rate_aging_S #> deriv(I_Young) <- rate_inf_Young - rate_rec_Young - rate_aging_I #> deriv(I_Old) <- rate_inf_Old - rate_rec_Old + rate_aging_I #> deriv(R_Young) <- rate_rec_Young - rate_aging_R #> deriv(R_Old) <- rate_rec_Old + rate_aging_R

title: Stratification with typed products
text: equivalent hand-written odin2 model: Running :
code: handwritten <- " inf_Young <- parameter() inf_Old <- parameter() rec_Young <- parameter() rec_Old <- parameter() aging_S <- parameter() aging_I <- parameter() aging_R <- parameter()  S_Young0 <- parameter() S_Old0 <- parameter() I_Young0 <- parameter() I_Old0 <- parameter() R_Young0 <- parameter() R_Old0 <- parameter()  initial(S_Young) <- S_Young0 initial(S_Old) <- S_Old0 initial(I_Young) <- I_Young0 initial(I_Old) <- I_Old0 initial(R_Young) <- R_Young0 initial(R_Old) <- R_Old0  rate_inf_Young <- inf_Young * S_Young * I_Young rate_inf_Old <- inf_Old * S_Old * I_Old rate_rec_Young <- rec_Young * I_Young rate_rec_Old <- rec_Old * I_Old rate_aging_S <- aging_S * S_Young rate_aging_I <- aging_I * I_Young rate_aging_R <- aging_R * R_Young  deriv(S_Young) <- -rate_inf_Young - rate_aging_S deriv(S_Old) <- -rate_inf_Old + rate_aging_S deriv(I_Young) <- rate_inf_Young - rate_rec_Young - rate_aging_I deriv(I_Old) <- rate_inf_Old - rate_rec_Old + rate_aging_I deriv(R_Young) <- title: Stratification with typed products
text: Scaling strata trivial. SIR × 3 age groups pairwise aging: 9 species 12 transitions—automatically generated correct stoichiometry.
code:
age3 <- labelled_petri_net(   c("Young", "Middle", "Old"),   aging_YM = "Young" %=>% "Middle",   aging_MO = "Middle" %=>% "Old" ) age3_typed <- typed_petri(age3, ont,   species_types    = c(Young = "Pop", Middle = "Pop", Old = "Pop"),   transition_types = c(aging_YM = "strata", aging_MO = "strata") ) age3_aug <- add_reflexives(age3_typed, list(   Young  = c("infect", "disease"),   Middle = c("infect", "disease"),   Old    = c("infect", "disease") ))  sir_age3 <- sir_aug %x% age3_aug cat("Species:", species_names(sir_age3), "
") #> Species: S_Young S_Middle S_Old I_Young I_Middle I_Old R_Young R_Middle R_Old cat("Transitions:", transition_names(sir_age3), "
") #> Transitions: inf_Young inf_Middle inf_Old rec_Young rec_Middle rec_Old aging_YM_S aging_MO_S aging_YM_I aging_MO_I aging_YM_R aging_MO_R cat("nparts: S =", acsets::nparts(sir_age3@pn, "S"),     ", T =", acsets::nparts(sir_age3@pn, "T"), "
") #> nparts: S = 9 , T = 12

title: Stratification with typed products
text: Risk groups follow pattern. stratify SIR two risk levels without movement groups (strata transitions): Without strata transitions, movement risk groups. infection recovery happen independently within group.
code: risk <- labelled_petri_net(c("High", "Low")) risk_typed <- typed_petri(risk, ont,   species_types    = c(High = "Pop", Low = "Pop"),   transition_types = setNames(character(0), character(0)) ) risk_aug <- add_reflexives(risk_typed, list(   High = c("infect", "disease"),   Low  = c("infect", "disease") ))  sir_risk <- sir_aug %x% risk_aug species_names(sir_risk) #> [1] "S_High" "S_Low"  "I_High" "I_Low"  "R_High" "R_Low" transition_names(sir_risk) #> [1] "inf_High" "inf_Low"  "rec_High" "rec_Low"

title: Stratification with typed products
text: stratify() alias typed_product():
code: sir_age2 <- stratify(sir_aug, age_aug) identical(species_names(sir_age2), species_names(sir_age)) #> [1] TRUE

title: Stratification with typed products
text: stratified Petri net can generate model type. age-stratified SIR stochastic model:
code: cat(pn_to_odin(sir_age, "stochastic")) #> ## Auto-generated by algebraicodin #>  #> ## Parameters #> inf_Young <- parameter() #> inf_Old <- parameter() #> rec_Young <- parameter() #> rec_Old <- parameter() #> aging_S <- parameter() #> aging_I <- parameter() #> aging_R <- parameter() #>  #> ## Initial conditions #> S_Young0 <- parameter() #> S_Old0 <- parameter() #> I_Young0 <- parameter() #> I_Old0 <- parameter() #> R_Young0 <- parameter() #> R_Old0 <- parameter() #>  #> initial(S_Young) <- S_Young0 #> initial(S_Old) <- S_Old0 #> initial(I_Young) <- I_Young0 #> initial(I_Old) <- I_Old0 #> initial(R_Young) <- R_Young0 #> initial(R_Old) <- R_Old0 #>  #> ## Transition probabilities and draws #> p_inf_Young <- 1 - exp(-(inf_Young * I_Young) * dt) #> n_inf_Young <- Binomial(S_Young, p_inf_Young) #> p_inf_Old <- 1 - exp(-(inf_Old * I_Old) * dt) #> n_inf_Old <- Binomial(S_Old, p_inf_Old) #> p_rec_Young <- 1 - exp(-(rec_Young) * dt) #> n_rec_Young <- Binomial(I_Young, p_rec_Young) #> p_rec_Old <- 1 - exp(-(rec_Old) * dt) #> n_rec_Old <- Binomial(I_Old, p_rec_Old) #> p_aging_S <- 1 - exp(-(aging_S) * dt) #> n_aging_S <- Binomial(S_Young, p_aging_S) #> p_aging_I <- 1 - exp(-(aging_I) * dt) #> n_aging_I <- Binomial(I_Young, p_aging_I) #> p_aging_R <- 1 - exp(-(aging_R) * dt) #> n_aging_R <- Binomial(R_Young, p_aging_R) #>  #> ## Update equations #> update(S_Young) <- S_Young - n_inf_Young - n_aging_S #> update(S_Old) <- S_Old - n_inf_Old + n_aging_S #> update(I_Young) <- I_Young + n_inf_Young - n_rec_Young - n_aging_I #> update(I_Old) <- I_Old + n_inf_Old - n_rec_Old + n_aging_I #> update(R_Young) <- R_Young + n_rec_Young - n_aging_R #> update(R_Old) <- R_Old + n_rec_Old + n_aging_R

title: Stratification with typed products
text: typed product guarantees structural correctness: every species pair consistent transitions, stoichiometry preserved. Scaling 2 20 strata requires changing strata model—base disease model unchanged.

title: Compositional Epidemiological Modeling with odin2
text: Compositional epidemic modeling using category theory. Build Petri nets stock-flow diagrams, compose via open systems undirected wiring diagrams (UWDs), stratify models age, geography, risk group, generate odin2/dust2 code high-performance simulation. R port AlgebraicPetri.jl StockFlow.jl AlgebraicJulia ecosystem.

title: Compositional Epidemiological Modeling with odin2
text: Install GitHub : algebraicodin depends catRgory companion packages acsets catlab, plus odin2 dust2 simulation.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("catRgory/algebraicodin")

title: Compositional Epidemiological Modeling with odin2
text: Define SIR model labelled Petri net, generate odin2 code, simulate:
code: library(algebraicodin)  # 1. Define an SIR Petri net # Transition names (inf, rec) become the odin2 rate parameter names sir <- labelled_petri_net(   c("S", "I", "R"),   inf = c("S", "I") %=>% c("I", "I"),   rec = "I" %=>% "R" )  # 2. Generate odin2 code code <- pn_to_odin(sir, type = "ode") cat(code)  # 3. Simulate with odin2/dust2 # Parameters: rate constants named after transitions (inf, rec) # plus initial conditions (S0, I0, R0) gen <- odin2::odin(code) sys <- dust2::dust_system_create(gen,   list(inf = 0.3, rec = 0.1, S0 = 990, I0 = 10, R0 = 0),   n_particles = 1) dust2::dust_system_set_state_initial(sys) t <- seq(0, 100) y <- dust2::dust_system_simulate(sys, t)  # 4. Plot results (y is [species x time] for n_particles = 1) sol <- data.frame(time = t, S = y[1, ], I = y[2, ], R = y[3, ]) plot_trajectory(sol, title = "SIR model")

title: Compositional Epidemiological Modeling with odin2
text: Petri nets — Define compartmental models bipartite graphs labelled_petri_net(), ReactionNet, %=>% transition operator. Stock-flow diagrams — Build models explicit stocks, flows, auxiliary variables, sum variables via stock_and_flow() flow(). Open systems & UWD composition — Expose boundary species, compose models undirected wiring diagrams: oapply(), %o%, compose(). Stratification — Typed Petri nets typed products (%x%) age, risk, geographic strata via infectious_ontology() stratify(). odin2 code generation — Generate ODE, DDE, stochastic, discrete-time odin2 code Petri nets (pn_to_odin()) stock-flow models (sf_to_odin()). Compositional dynamics — ResourceSharer Machine open dynamical systems simulation without code generation. Bayesian fitting — Observation models, dust2 particle filtering, MCMC via monty fit_mcmc(). Epi building blocks — Pre-built components infection, recovery, vaccination, waning, via epi_dict() compose_epi(). Array-based codegen — Efficient array-indexed odin2 code large stratified models. Spatial models — Multi-patch dynamics sf_spatial().

title: Add reflexive (self-loop) transitions for stratification — add_reflexives
code: sir <- labelled_petri_net(   c("S", "I", "R"),   inf = c("S", "I") %=>% c("I", "I"),   rec = "I" %=>% "R" ) onto <- infectious_ontology() tp <- typed_petri(sir, onto,   species_types = c(S = "Pop", I = "Pop", R = "Pop"),   transition_types = c(inf = "infect", rec = "disease") ) # Add strata-type reflexives for each species tp_refl <- add_reflexives(tp, list(   S = "strata", I = "strata", R = "strata" )) transition_names(tp_refl) #> [1] "inf"           "rec"           "refl_S_strata" "refl_I_strata" #> [5] "refl_R_strata"

what: algebraicodin: Compositional Epidemiological Modeling with 'odin2' — algebraicodin-package
title: algebraicodin: Compositional Epidemiological Modeling with 'odin2' — algebraicodin-package
text: Compositional model building using applied category theory, targeting odin2 DSL high-performance simulation via dust2. Supports Petri nets, stock-flow diagrams, open systems, composition via undirected wiring diagrams. Generates deterministic (ODE) stochastic (discrete-time) odin2 code. Port AlgebraicPetri.jl StockFlow.jl AlgebraicJulia ecosystem.

text: ninputs Number input ports nstates Number internal states (inferred dynamics_expr omitted) noutputs Number output ports (inferred readout_expr omitted) dynamics Function (u, x, p, t) -> du. Auto-built dynamics_expr omitted. readout Function (u, p, t) -> y. Auto-built readout_expr omitted. state_names Optional state names dynamics_expr Optional named list quoted expressions per state. Use input_1, input_2, etc. input port references. readout_expr Optional list quoted expressions, one per output port params Character vector parameter names

title: Create a ContinuousMachine — continuous_machine
code: # Damped harmonic oscillator with external forcing osc <- continuous_machine(   ninputs = 1,   dynamics_expr = list(     x = quote(v),     v = quote(-k * x - c * v + input_1)   ),   readout_expr = list(quote(x)),   params = c("k", "c") ) osc@ninputs    # 1 #> [1] 1 osc@nstates    # 2 #> [1] 2 osc@noutputs   # 1 #> [1] 1

title: Generate odin2 code from a Petri net — pn_to_odin
code: pn_to_odin(   pn,   type = c("ode", "dde", "stochastic", "discrete"),   rate_style = "mass_action",   delays = NULL,   compare = NULL )  to_odin(   pn,   type = c("ode", "dde", "stochastic", "discrete"),   rate_style = "mass_action",   delays = NULL,   compare = NULL )

title: Generate odin2 code from a Petri net — pn_to_odin
text: pn Petri net ACSet (LabelledPetriNet ReactionNet) type Model type: "ode": continuous ODE deriv() "dde": delay differential equation deriv() + delay() "stochastic": discrete-time stochastic update() + Binomial() "discrete": discrete-time deterministic Euler update() rate_style Currently "mass_action" implemented. "custom" reserved error requested. delays Named list DDE: maps transition names delay specs. element list tau (numeric delay time) optionally species (character vector input species delayed). compare Optional named list mapping observed variables distribution families (e.g., list(cases = "Poisson"))

title: Generate odin2 code from a Petri net — pn_to_odin
text: character string odin2 DSL code

title: Generate odin2 code from a Petri net — pn_to_odin
code: sir <- labelled_petri_net(   c("S", "I", "R"),   inf = c("S", "I") %=>% c("I", "I"),   rec = "I" %=>% "R" ) # Generate ODE code code <- pn_to_odin(sir, type = "ode") cat(code) #> ## Auto-generated by algebraicodin #>  #> ## Parameters #> inf <- parameter() #> rec <- parameter() #>  #> ## Initial conditions #> S0 <- parameter() #> I0 <- parameter() #> R0 <- parameter() #>  #> initial(S) <- S0 #> initial(I) <- I0 #> initial(R) <- R0 #>  #> ## Transition rates #> rate_inf <- inf * S * I #> rate_rec <- rec * I #>  #> ## Derivatives #> deriv(S) <- -rate_inf #> deriv(I) <- rate_inf - rate_rec #> deriv(R) <- rate_rec  # Generate stochastic discrete-time code code_stoch <- pn_to_odin(sir, type = "stochastic") cat(code_stoch) #> ## Auto-generated by algebraicodin #>  #> ## Parameters #> inf <- parameter() #> rec <- parameter() #>  #> ## Initial conditions #> S0 <- parameter() #> I0 <- parameter() #> R0 <- parameter() #>  #> initial(S) <- S0 #> initial(I) <- I0 #> initial(R) <- R0 #>  #> ## Transition probabilities and draws #> p_inf <- 1 - exp(-(inf * I) * dt) #> n_inf <- Binomial(S, p_inf) #> p_rec <- 1 - exp(-(rec) * dt) #> n_rec <- Binomial(I, p_rec) #>  #> ## Update equations #> update(S) <- S - n_inf #> update(I) <- I + n_inf - n_rec #> update(R) <- R + n_rec

title: User-friendly stratification wrapper — stratify
text: Computes typed product two TypedPetriNets. Equivalent typed_product(base, strata).

title: User-friendly stratification wrapper — stratify
code: stratify(base, strata)

title: User-friendly stratification wrapper — stratify
text: base TypedPetriNet (base model, e.g. SIR) strata TypedPetriNet (stratification model, e.g. age groups)

title: User-friendly stratification wrapper — stratify
text: TypedPetriNet (stratified model)

title: User-friendly stratification wrapper — stratify
code: # Same as typed_product(); see that function for a full example onto <- infectious_ontology() sir <- labelled_petri_net(   c("S", "I", "R"),   inf = c("S", "I") %=>% c("I", "I"),   rec = "I" %=>% "R" ) tp_sir <- typed_petri(sir, onto,   species_types = c(S = "Pop", I = "Pop", R = "Pop"),   transition_types = c(inf = "infect", rec = "disease") ) tp_sir <- add_reflexives(tp_sir, list(   S = "strata", I = "strata", R = "strata" ))  ages <- labelled_petri_net(c("young", "old"), aging = "young" %=>% "old") tp_ages <- typed_petri(ages, onto,   species_types = c(young = "Pop", old = "Pop"),   transition_types = c(aging = "strata") ) tp_ages <- add_reflexives(tp_ages, list(   young = c("infect", "disease"), old = c("infect", "disease") ))  result <- stratify(tp_sir, tp_ages) species_names(result) #> [1] "S_young" "S_old"   "I_young" "I_old"   "R_young" "R_old"

what: Compute the typed product (stratification) of typed Petri nets — typed_product
title: Compute the typed product (stratification) of typed Petri nets — typed_product
text: core stratification operation. Given two typed Petri nets sharing type system, typed product creates new Petri net whose species pairs species matching types, whose transitions pairs transitions matching types.

title: Compute the typed product (stratification) of typed Petri nets — typed_product
code: typed_product(tp1, tp2)

title: Compute the typed product (stratification) of typed Petri nets — typed_product
text: tp1, tp2 TypedPetriNet objects type system

title: Compute the typed product (stratification) of typed Petri nets — typed_product
text: TypedPetriNet (stratified model)

title: Compute the typed product (stratification) of typed Petri nets — typed_product
code: # Stratify SIR by two age groups onto <- infectious_ontology()  sir <- labelled_petri_net(   c("S", "I", "R"),   inf = c("S", "I") %=>% c("I", "I"),   rec = "I" %=>% "R" ) tp_sir <- typed_petri(sir, onto,   species_types = c(S = "Pop", I = "Pop", R = "Pop"),   transition_types = c(inf = "infect", rec = "disease") ) tp_sir <- add_reflexives(tp_sir, list(   S = "strata", I = "strata", R = "strata" ))  ages <- labelled_petri_net(c("young", "old"), aging = "young" %=>% "old") tp_ages <- typed_petri(ages, onto,   species_types = c(young = "Pop", old = "Pop"),   transition_types = c(aging = "strata") ) tp_ages <- add_reflexives(tp_ages, list(   young = c("infect", "disease"), old = c("infect", "disease") ))  result <- typed_product(tp_sir, tp_ages) species_names(result) # e.g. S_young, S_old, I_young, ... #> [1] "S_young" "S_old"   "I_young" "I_old"   "R_young" "R_old"

title: algebraicodin 0.1.0
text: Petri net types: PetriNet, LabelledPetriNet, ReactionNet built acsets ACSet framework. Declarative Petri net builder via labelled_petri_net() %=>% transition operator. Stoichiometry via transition_matrices() pure-R mass-action vectorfield() compatible deSolve.

title: algebraicodin 0.1.0
text: Full stock-flow schema (SchStockFlow) stocks, flows, auxiliary variables, sum variables, parameters, typed links. Declarative builder stock_and_flow() flow() helper. Stock-flow vectorfield sf_vectorfield() simulation simulate_sf(). Stock-flow transition matrices via sf_transition_matrices().

title: algebraicodin 0.1.0
text: Open Petri nets exposed legs compositional assembly. UWD-based composition via oapply() name-based compose_open(). Infix operators %o% (composition) %x% (stratification). Open stock-flow models via open_stock_flow() sf_oapply(). Bridge Open catlab::StructuredCospan via as_structured_cospan() / as_open().

title: algebraicodin 0.1.0
text: TypedPetriNet morphisms type system (ontology). typed_petri() assigning species/transition types. typed_product() / stratify() categorical product slice category. add_reflexives() identity transitions needed stratification. infectious_ontology() providing standard epi type system. Stock-flow stratification via sf_stratify(), sf_typed_product(), sf_spatial() spatial/contact-structured models.

title: algebraicodin 0.1.0
text: pn_to_odin() / to_odin(): generate odin2 DSL code Petri nets supporting ODE, DDE, stochastic (Binomial), discrete (Euler) model types. sf_to_odin(): generate odin2 code stock-flow models. sf_to_odin_array(): compact array-based code generation stratified stock-flow models. System constructors pn_to_odin_system(), sf_to_odin_system(), sf_to_odin_array_system() returning callable odin2 generators. Observation/comparison blocks via observe() Bayesian fitting.

title: algebraicodin 0.1.0
text: ResourceSharer undirected open dynamical systems (UWD-composable). Machine directed open dynamical systems (DWD-composable). Continuous, discrete, delay system types. Composition via oapply_dynam() oapply_machine(). petri_to_continuous(), petri_to_discrete(), petri_to_delay() converters. euler_approx() continuous--discrete conversion. rs_to_odin() machine_to_odin() code generation dynamical systems.

title: algebraicodin 0.1.0
text: spontaneous_petri() exposure_petri() common epi transitions. epi_dict() pre-built dictionary: infection, recovery, vaccination, waning, death, exposure, progression, hospitalization. compose_epi() assembling epi models UWDs dictionaries.

title: algebraicodin 0.1.0
text: fit_mcmc() MCMC fitting via monty dust2. Prior specification helpers: prior_uniform(), prior_normal(), prior_lognormal(), prior_exp(), build_prior(). Diagnostic plots: plot_traces(), plot_posterior(), plot_fit().

title: algebraicodin 0.1.0
text: plot_petri(), plot_stock_flow(), plot_uwd() using DiagrammeR. plot_trajectory(), plot_comparison(), plot_diff() simulation output.

title: algebraicodin 0.1.0
text: Imports: acsets, catlab, cli, rlang, S7. Suggests: odin2, dust2, deSolve, monty, DiagrammeR, ggplot2, tidyr.
